tool_input.prompt:
Find the session trace span view in this Vue 3 frontend codebase at /Users/user/project/. The user wants to change the trace span display so that only "first-class" spans are shown by default (folded), and nested child spans can be unfolded when clicked.

Please find:
1. The Vue component(s) that render the trace span tree/list (likely something like TraceView, SpanTree, SessionTrace, etc.)
2. The data structure used for spans (what distinguishes "first-class" spans from children/nested spans - look for parent/child relationships, span_id, parent_span_id, or similar)
3. Any existing folding/expand UI patterns in that component
4. The API endpoint that serves the trace data (in web/app.py)

Report with file paths and line numbers. Under 300 words. Include a brief snippet of the relevant render logic so I know what to edit.

content.text: Perfect! I have all the information needed. Let me compile the findings. ## Summary **Vue Component (Frontend):** - **File:** `/Users/user/project/src/views/SessionTraceView.vue` - **Lines 13-31:** TreeTable with `expandedKeys` state management. Currently expands ALL groups by default (line 20-31). - **Lines 194-251:** TreeTable rendering using PrimeVue TreeTable component with expander column. **Key Render Logic (lines 202-217):** ```vue
